description: Vite + React (or Vue/Solid) SPA for an auth-walled application. Login → app shell loads once → client routing. No SEO. Heavier JS bundle is acceptable because users come back. Internal tools, dashboards, B2B apps.

anti_recommendations.no-code-splitting: kill — single bundle for a multi-route SPA = unusable on slow networks
anti_recommendations.ssr-on-spa-only-surface: warn — adds infra cost with no SEO benefit
anti_recommendations.redux-without-justification: warn — TanStack Query handles server state; Zustand handles UI state
anti_recommendations.context-as-global-state: kill — re-renders cascade; use Zustand/Jotai for global UI state

canon_references: Evan You + Vite team, Vite docs (2020-2024)
TanStack Query docs — server state vs UI state distinction
Marcy Sutton, Accessibility in JavaScript Applications (2017+)
